name: GT-orderunit-def
source: HOS, refs/hos/joa-m.md:368-372 (1.2.1, definition of order-unit space and order norm), VERBATIM: "An element  $e \in A^+$  is called an order unit for A if for all  $a \in A$  there is  $\lambda > 0$  such that  $-\lambda e \le a \le \lambda e$ . We say A is Archimedean if  $na \le e$  for all  $n \in \mathbb{N}$ —the natural numbers—implies  $a \le 0$ . In this case A has a norm given by" "$$||a|| = \inf\{\lambda > 0: -\lambda e \le a \le \lambda e\}.$$" "A is said to be an order unit space if A has an order unit and A is Archimedean." Fixes the order-norm formula ||a||=inf{t>0:-t e<=a<=t e} (def-order-unit-space). Also gives the order-unit-norm fact: -||x|| 1 <= x <= ||x|| 1 for any x (taking t=||x|| in the inf), hence x >= -||x|| 1.
